MfgProcess: Automated progressive die stamping + Robotic MAG welding
Constraints: Heavy unsprung mass penalty; requires zinc-nickel e-coating to prevent severe rust

MfgProcess: Low-pressure die casting (LPDC) + T6 heat treatment
Constraints: Prone to micro-porosity; requires strict X-ray inspection; lower fatigue limit

MfgProcess: Cold rolling + Multi-stage stamping + automated welding
Constraints: Highest weight penalty; highly susceptible to rust; requires heavy paint/coating

MfgProcess: LPDC + slow cooling + CNC face turning
Constraints: Slower cycle times than standard casting; prone to thermal shrinkage defects
Lithium reactivity requires specialized foundry handling; high raw material cost

MfgProcess: Powder metallurgy + Hot extrusion + Diamond-tool machining
Constraints: Extremely abrasive to cutting tools; low elongation (brittle); unrepairable

MfgProcess: Billet forging + CNC milling + T83 heat treatment
Constraints: Anisotropic grain structure; high scrap cost during machining phase

MfgProcess: Closed-die hydraulic forging + CNC machining + T6 aging
Constraints: High upfront die tooling costs; significant material waste during CNC phase

MfgProcess: Specialty casting or forging + precipitation hardening
Constraints: Scandium supply is strictly limited and extremely expensive; geopolitical risks

MfgProcess: Low-pressure casting + Rotary flow-forming over a mandrel
Constraints: Geometrically limited to symmetrical barrel shapes; cannot form complex spokes
Extreme tool wear; very slow material removal rates; cost-prohibitive

MfgProcess: Forged billet + CNC machining with high-pressure coolant
Constraints: Reactive at high temperatures; requires carbide tools; massive manufacturing cost
Cannot be repaired if cracked; difficult to recycle; catastrophic brittle failure
High springback during forming; galling issues during machining
Extremely high strength makes machining incredibly slow and expensive

MfgProcess: Prepreg hand layup + Vacuum bagging + Autoclave curing
Constraints: Labor intensive; 4+ hour curing cycle makes mass production impossible
Kevlar absorbs moisture if matrix is damaged; difficult to trim cleanly
Severe fire hazard during machining (magnesium dust); requires galvanic isolation
Extreme work-hardening makes CNC machining almost impossible; massive overkill

MfgProcess: Forging + Quench and Temper heat treatment
Constraints: Heavy; requires continuous rust protection; hard to weld compared to low-carbon
Requires strict heat control to prevent cracking; heavy mass penalty
Astronomical cost; completely unnecessary for automotive applications

MfgProcess: Closed die forging + Overaging heat treatment
Constraints: Susceptible to stress corrosion cracking if heat treatment is flawed; cannot be welded

MfgProcess: Extrusion or Forging + Solution heat treatment
Constraints: Poor corrosion resistance (contains copper); requires alclad or heavy anodizing

MfgProcess: Sand casting with protective SF6 gas
Constraints: Toxic foundry gases required; highly susceptible to galvanic corrosion
Easily dents; requires micro-arc oxidation coating to survive road salt
Heavy for a composite; too brittle for pothole impacts; slow factory cycle time
Fibers fuzz during machining; degrades rapidly in UV light if clear-coated
Very dense/heavy; thermal conductivity is poor; overkill for rims
High density; machining requires rigid setups to prevent chatter
Hyper-expensive aerospace steel; completely unviable for automotive budgets
Rapid degradation from moisture and UV light; loses strength over time

MfgProcess: Specialty casting + Machining in controlled vents
Constraints: Beryllium dust is highly toxic/carcinogenic; illegal for consumer car parts
Creeps under constant vehicle load; absorbs water which drops yield strength
Far too flexible; will warp under vehicle weight and brake heat
Melts under brake caliper heat; shatters in freezing temperatures
Shards like glass at room temperature; impossible to cold-form
Used for chemical reactors; density is too high; extreme machining difficulty
Extremely heavy; yield strength is dangerously low for automotive impacts
Material does not exist at commercial scale; strictly prototype/hypercar use
